repositories.name: cf-stats
repositories.readme:
# Codeforces Stat Visualization
# Installation ### For step-by-step screenshots go [here](docs/INSTALLATIONSTEPS.md) ### Video Demonstration - [Hindi](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lPASqH0ZoIc) 1. Star this repository :pray: 2. Create a copy of this repository by clicking [here](https://github.com/sudiptob2/cf-stats/generate). Note: this is **not** the same as forking a copy because it copies everything fresh, without the huge commit history. 3. Goto [config/.env.template](config/.env.template) and put your codeforces handle in the `CF_HANDLE` key. 4. Go to the [Actions Page](../../actions?query=workflow%3A"Generate+Stats+Images") and press "Run Workflow" on the right side of the screen to generate images for the first time. - The images will be automatically regenerated every 24 hours, but they can be regenerated manually by running the workflow this way. 5. Take a look at the images that have been created in the [`output`](output) folder. 6. To add your statistics to your GitHub Profile README, copy and paste the following lines of code into your markdown content. Change the `your-github-username` value to your GitHub username. ```md ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-github-username/cf-stats/main/output/light_card.svg#gh-dark-mode-only) ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/your-github-username/cf-stats/main/output/light_card.svg) ``` ```md ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sudiptob2/cf-stats/main/output/max_rating.svg) ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sudiptob2/cf-stats/main/output/rating.svg) ``` # Contribution guideline If you like this project and want to improve by adding features, fixing bugs or anything, please follow the [contributing guidelines](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md). # Acknowledgments ### Contributors List - [Sudipto](https://github.com/sudiptob2) - [Nazmul](https://github.com/nazmulweb) - [Manish](https://github.com/csemanish12) ### Inspiration This project is heavily inspired by [github-stats](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats) project

repositories.name: Interpreter
repositories.readme:
# ASM-PyPas рҹҡҖ

ASM-PyPas es un intГ©rprete experimental con interfaz web. El proyecto combina:

- рҹҢҗ Backend en Flask para servir la interfaz y exponer una API.
- рҹ§  Motor de interpretaciГіn en Python (tokenizaciГіn, estructuras y ejecuciГіn bГЎsica).
- рҹ“қ Editor web con CodeMirror para crear, guardar y ejecutar archivos dentro de la carpeta codes.

## вңЁ Estado actual

Actualmente puedes:

- рҹ“Ғ Crear y eliminar archivos de cГіdigo desde la UI.
- рҹ”„ Cargar archivos existentes al abrir la aplicaciГіn.
- рҹ’ҫ Guardar cambios automГЎticamente cada segundo cuando detecta modificaciones.
- в–¶пёҸ Ejecutar cГіdigo vГӯa API y visualizar resultado/errores en la terminal integrada.

## рҹҸ—пёҸ Arquitectura

- вҡҷпёҸ main.py: arranque del servidor Flask.
- рҹ§© modules/interpreter: lГіgica del lenguaje (tokens, expresiones, estructuras y ejecuciГіn).
- рҹ”Ң modules/web: rutas web, API, validaciones de payload y utilidades.
- рҹ–ҘпёҸ gui: frontend estГЎtico (HTML, CSS, JS, CodeMirror).
- рҹ“Ӯ codes: archivos fuente editables desde la interfaz.

## рҹҢі Estructura del repositorio

```text
asm-pypas/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.py
в”ңв”Җв”Җ README.md
в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt
в”ңв”Җв”Җ codes/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ docs/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ gui/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ modules/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ generic/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ interpreter/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ web/
в””в”Җв”Җ scripts/
```

## рҹ“Ӣ Requisitos

- рҹҗҚ Python 3.10+ (recomendado).
- рҹ“Ұ pip.

Dependencias actuales en requirements.txt:

- вң… flask

## рҹӣ пёҸ InstalaciГіn

```bash
git clone https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/Interpreter.git
cd Interpreter

python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## в–¶пёҸ EjecuciГіn

OpciГіn 1 (directo):

```bash
python3 main.py
```

OpciГіn 2 (script):

```bash
bash scripts/run.sh
```

El servidor inicia en:

- рҹҢҚ http://127.0.0.1:8000
- рҹҢҚ http://localhost:8000

## рҹ”„ Flujo de uso

1. Abre la aplicaciГіn en el navegador.
2. Crea un archivo nuevo con el botГіn +.
3. Escribe cГіdigo en el editor.
4. El contenido se guarda automГЎticamente en segundo plano.
5. Ejecuta con el botГіn Play para ver salida y errores en la terminal inferior.

## рҹ”— API disponible

Rutas principales expuestas por Flask:

- GET /: interfaz principal.
- GET /gui/<subpath>: recursos estГЎticos de la UI.
- POST /api/run: ejecuta el archivo actual. Body JSON: {"name": string, "code": string}.
- POST /api/save: guarda el archivo actual. Body JSON: {"name": string, "code": string}.
- POST /api/getcode?name=<archivo>: obtiene el contenido de un archivo.
- GET /api/getcodes?name=<archivo>: obtiene contenido de un archivo.
- GET /api/initcodes: lista nombres disponibles en codes.
- GET /api/newcode?name=<archivo>: crea entrada de archivo vacГӯa.
- GET /api/delcurr?name=<archivo>: elimina un archivo.

Notas tГ©cnicas:

- рҹӣЎпёҸ Se valida nombre de archivo para prevenir path traversal.
- рҹ“Ҹ TamaГұo mГЎximo de cГіdigo por request: 128 MB.

## рҹ§° Scripts Гәtiles

- рҹҡҖ scripts/run.sh: ejecuta la app con optimizaciones y luego limpia temporales.
- рҹ§№ scripts/clean.sh: elimina archivos auxiliares log/aux y __pycache__.
- рҹ“ҡ scripts/builddocs.sh: compila documentos LaTeX en docs y limpia temporales.

## рҹ“– DocumentaciГіn

La documentaciГіn tГ©cnica del lenguaje/proyecto estГЎ en la carpeta docs.

## вҡ пёҸ Limitaciones actuales

- El intГ©rprete estГЎ en evoluciГіn y no cubre todas las construcciones de un lenguaje completo.
- Algunos mensajes de error y resultados aГәn estГЎn orientados a depuraciГіn.
- No hay suite formal de tests automatizados incluida en este repositorio.

repositories.name: Jamazon
repositories.readme:
# Jamazon - Event Manager рҹ“…рҹҡҖ

**Jamazon** (also known as *Dynamic Events*) is a desktop application developed in Python for the efficient management of events and resources. It uses a modern graphical interface and advanced data structures to handle scheduling and avoid resource conflicts.

## рҹҢҹ Key Features

* **Task Management:** Easily create and delete tasks.
* **Event Definition:** Schedule events with specific date and time ranges.
* **Resource Management:** Add resources and handle dependencies between them.
* **Conflict Detection:** Intelligent system that checks if necessary resources are available and do not conflict with other rules.
* **Modern Interface:** Dark and friendly GUI based on `customtkinter`.

## рҹӣ пёҸ Installation and Execution

Follow these steps to run the application in your local environment:

1. **Clone the repository:**

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/Jamazon.git
   cd Jamazon
   ```

2. **Install dependencies:**
   Make sure you have Python installed. Then, install the necessary libraries:

   ```bash
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```

3. **Run the application:**

   ```bash
   python main.py
   ```

## рҹ“– Usage Guide

Upon starting the application, you will see a control panel with the following options:

* **Create new task:** Opens a form to register a new task in the system.
* **Remove existing task:** Allows deleting tasks that are no longer needed.
* **Add Resource:** Adds new resources (e.g., rooms, equipment) that can be assigned to events.
* **Define new event:** Creates complex events that require resources and have a specific duration. The system will automatically validate if resources are available and if there are dependency conflicts.

## рҹ§  Technical Details and Modules

The project is built with a modular approach. The main components are detailed below:

### 1. Core (`modules/`)

* **`app` (in `main.py`)**:
  * It is the main window of the application.
  * Configures the interface, loads images, and manages navigation to other tools.

* **`Calendar` (in `modules/calendar.py`)**:
  * Manages the list of active events and used resources.
  * Handles saving and loading the application state.
  * Coordinates task availability.

* **`event` (in `modules/events.py`)**:
  * Represents an individual event with date, time, and necessary resources.
  * **Validation:** Checks dependencies and resource collisions upon initialization. If there is a conflict, it prevents the event creation.

### 2. Graphical Interface (`modules/gui_core/`)

* **`EventCreator` (in `EventDefiner.py`)**: Window to enter details for new events.
* **`TaskCreator`**: Interface to register new tasks in the system.
* **`TaskRemover`**: Interface to delete existing tasks.
* **`ResAdder`**: Allows adding new resources to the database (`resources.json`).

## рҹ“Ӯ Project Structure

```text
Jamazon/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ changelog           # Project change log
в”ңв”Җв”Җ clean.py            # Script to clean temporary files
в”ңв”Җв”Җ logs.txt            # Error and event log file
в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.py             # рҹҸҒ Main entry point of the application
в”ңв”Җв”Җ README.md           # Project documentation
в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt    # List of Python dependencies
в”ңв”Җв”Җ test.py             # Script for quick tests
в”ңв”Җв”Җ modules/            # рҹ§  Logical core of the system
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ calendar.py     # Calendar logic and availability
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ events.py       # Event class definition and validations
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ gvar.py         # Global variables
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ iohandler.py    # Base handlers and utilities
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ SegTree.py      # Segment Tree implementation
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ utils.py        # General utility functions
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ gui_core/       # рҹҺЁ Graphical Interface Components
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ EventDefiner.py   # Window to define new events
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ Showers.py        # Event viewer
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ ResAdder.py       # Window to add resources
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ TaskCreator.py    # Window to create tasks
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ TaskRemover.py    # Window to delete tasks
в”ңв”Җв”Җ saved/              # Folder for saved data
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/          # рҹ“„ Templates and static data
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ resources.json  # Database of available resources
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ tasks.json      # Database of saved tasks
в””в”Җв”Җ tests/              # рҹ§Ә Unit tests
```

## рҹӨқ Contribution

Contributions are welcome! If you want to improve Jamazon:

1. Fork the project.
2. Create a branch for your new feature (`git checkout -b feature/new-feature`).
3. Make your changes and commit (`git commit -m "Add new feature"`).
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/new-feature`).
5. Open a Pull Request.

## Star History

[![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=VIRUSGAMING64/Jamazon&type=date&legend=top-left)](https://www.star-history.com/#VIRUSGAMING64/Jamazon&type=date&legend=top-left)

---
Developed by [VIRUSGAMING64](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64)

repositories.name: MPTB_vshell
repositories.description: Is an new modular python-telegram-bot version of vshell
repositories.readme:
# рҹӨ– MPTB_vshell

**MPTB_vshell** is a robust and modular Telegram bot framework designed to integrate the power of **OpenAI (ChatGPT)** with advanced media management capabilities. Its flexible architecture allows for easy extension and customization.

## рҹҡҖ Key Features

- **рҹӨ– Deep Telegram Integration**: Built on `python-telegram-bot` for fluid and reactive interaction.
- **рҹ§  Artificial Intelligence**: Native support for **OpenAI (ChatGPT)**, enabling natural conversations and language processing.
- **рҹ“Ӯ Advanced Media Management**: Download and upload large files with `Pyrogram` efficiency.
- **рҹҺ¬ Media Compression**: Built-in video compression capabilities leveraging **FFmpeg** (H.265/HEVC).
- **рҹҢҗ Web Interface**: Integrated Flask-based web dashboard for file management (`web/app.py`).
- **рҹҗі Docker Ready**: Includes a configured `Dockerfile` with all dependencies and optimizations (jemalloc).
- **рҹ‘Ө User & Role System**: Complete management of users, states, and permissions (Admin, User, Banned, etc.).
- **рҹ§© Modular Architecture**: Code organized into independent modules (`brain`, `core`, `downup`, `compress`, etc.) for easy maintenance and scalability.
- **рҹ’ҫ Data Persistence**: Simple yet effective database system to maintain state.

## рҹ“Ӣ Requirements

- рҹҗҚ **Python 3.8** or higher.
- рҹҺ¬ **FFmpeg** (Required for media processing).
- рҹ“ұ A **Telegram** account and a **Bot Token** (obtained from @BotFather).
- рҹ”‘ **Telegram API ID** & **API Hash** (for Pyrogram, get it from my.telegram.org).
- рҹӨ– **OpenAI API Key** (optional, for AI features).

## рҹӣ пёҸ Installation

### Option A: Local Installation

1. **Clone the repository:**
   ```bash
   git clone <repository-url>
   cd MPTB_vshell
   ```

2. **Create a virtual environment (recommended):**
   ```bash
   python -m venv .venv
   source .venv/bin/activate  # On Linux/Mac
   # .venv\Scripts\activate  # On Windows
   ```

3. **Install dependencies:**
   ```bash
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```
   *Note: Ensure `ffmpeg` is installed on your system if you plan to use media features.*

### Option B: Docker (Recommended)

1. **Build the image:**
   ```bash
   docker build -t mptb_vshell .
   ```

2. **Run the container:**
   ```bash
   docker run -d --env-file .env --name mptb_vshell mptb_vshell
   ```

## вҡҷпёҸ Configuration

The bot uses environment variables for configuration. You can set them in your system, create a `.env` file, or pass them to Docker.

Required variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `TOKEN` | Telegram Bot Token. |
| `API_ID` | Telegram Application ID. |
| `API_HASH` | Telegram API Hash. |
| `OPEN_AI` | OpenAI API Key. |
| `ADMIN` | Numerical ID of the main administrator. |
| `HTTP_PROXY` | (Optional) HTTP Proxy. |
| `HTTPS_PROXY` | (Optional) HTTPS Proxy. |

## в–¶пёҸ Usage

Once configured, start the bot with the following command:

```bash
python bot.py
```

## рҹ“Ӯ Project Structure

```text
MPTB_vshell/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ bot.py                  # рҹҡҖ Main bot entry point
в”ңв”Җв”Җ clean.sh                # рҹ§№ Script to clean temporary files
в”ңв”Җв”Җ database.csv            # рҹ’ҫ Simple database (CSV)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Dockerfile              # рҹҗі Docker configuration
в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt        # рҹ“Ұ Dependency list
в”ңв”Җв”Җ modules/                # рҹ§© Modular bot logic
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ brain.py            # рҹ§  Brain: Message processing
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ chatgpt.py          # рҹӨ– AI: OpenAI integration
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ database.py         # рҹ—„пёҸ DB: Database handling
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ gvar.py             # вҡҷпёҸ Config: Global variables & env
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ utils.py            # рҹӣ пёҸ Utils: Various tools
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ compress/           # рҹҺ¬ Compress: Video compression logic
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ core/               # вҡЎ Core: Commands, queues, and workers
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ downup/             # рҹ“Ҙрҹ“Ө DownUp: Media download & upload
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ entity/             # рҹ‘Ө Entity: Object definitions (User, etc.)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ fuse/               # рҹ”Ң Fuse: Additional/experimental modules
в””в”Җв”Җ web/                    # рҹҢҗ Web: Administration web interface
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ app.py              # рҹҢҗ Flask application
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ static/             # рҹҺЁ Static assets
    в””в”Җв”Җ templates/          # рҹ“„ HTML templates
```

---

# рҹ“ҡ Detailed Technical Documentation

This section provides a deep dive into the architecture, design patterns, and internal data flow of **MPTB_vshell**, intended for developers who wish to understand or extend the system.

## вҡҷпёҸ Architecture & Core Components

### 1. The Kernel (`bot.py`)
The `bot.py` file serves as the system's entry point and orchestrator. It manages concurrent execution using Python's `threading` module to ensure the bot remains responsive while performing heavy tasks:
- **Bot Application**: Initializes the `python-telegram-bot` application and starts the polling loop to listen for updates.
- **Database Saver**: A daemon thread that periodically (every `DB_SAVE_TIMEOUT` seconds) commits the in-memory database state to the `database.csv` file on disk, ensuring data persistence without blocking the main event loop.
- **Web Server**: Launches the Flask application (`web/app.py`) in a separate thread. This allows the admin dashboard to run side-by-side with the Telegram bot process.
- **Initialization Routine**: The `__init__` function waits for the bot's event loop to be ready and verifies that the configured administrators (`ADMINS_ID`) are properly registered in the database with the correct privileges (`ADMIN | LLM`).

### 2. The "Brain" (`modules/brain.py`)
This module acts as the central **Message Router**. When an update (Message) is received, `brain.py` analyzes its content and delegates responsibility:
- **Command Handling**: If the text starts with a slash `/` (or the configured `BOT_HANDLER`), it matches the command against the registry in `modules/core/commands.py` and executes the corresponding function.
- **Media Detection**: It scans messages for URLs from supported platforms (like YouTube, Instagram, Facebook). If a match is found, it triggers the extraction and download logic in `modules/downup`.
- **AI Processing**: If the message is not a command or media link, and the user possesses the `LLM` flag authorization, the text is forwarded to `modules/chatgpt.py` or configured LLM provider for natural language processing.

### 3. Database System (`modules/database.py`)
The project uses a lightweight, custom **CSV-based** persistence layer designed for speed and simplicity.
- **In-Memory Cache**: Upon startup, the entire CSV file is loaded into a dictionary (`self.dic`). This ensures that user lookups are typically O(1) operations, extremely fast.
- **Serialization**: The `database.py` module handles the conversion between `peer` (User) objects and their CSV string representation.
- **Thread-Safety**: While simple, the `save()` method is called from a dedicated thread, minimizing I/O blocking on the main execution path.

### 4. Roles, Permissions & Bitmasks (`modules/core/enums.py` & `entity`)
To manage permissions efficiently, the system utilizes **Bitwise Flags**. This allows for complex role combinations within a single integer field.
- **Role Flags**:
    - `ADMIN` (16): Full system control.
    - `LLM` (8): Authorized to interact with the AI models.
    - `BANNED` (1): Restricted access.
- **Message Type Flags**: Used to quickly categorize content type (VIDEO, DOCUMENT, URL, etc.) internally.
- **User State**: A user's state is simply the sum of these flags (e.g., `ADMIN | LLM` = 24). Checks are performed using bitwise AND operations (e.g., `if user.state & ADMIN:`).

### 5. Media Engine (`modules/downup`)
This module handles the heavy lifting of file operations, specifically designed to bypass standard bot API limits where possible.
- **`downloader.py`**: A custom **Multi-threaded HTTP Downloader**. It requests files in "chunks" (byte ranges) concurrently, saturating the available bandwidth for faster downloads.
- **Pyrogram Integration**: For uploading files larger than 50MB (up to 2GB or 4GB for premium), the bot leverages `Pyrogram` (a MTProto client) instead of the standard HTTP Bot API. This requires the `API_ID` and `API_HASH` credentials.

### 6. Video Compression (`modules/compress`)
Automated media optimization pipeline using **FFmpeg**.
- **HEVC/H.265**: The default encoding profile targets `libx265`, providing significant size reduction (often 50%+) compared to standard H.264 while maintaining visual quality.
- **Concurrency**: The compressor class manages FFmpeg processes, potentially running them in separate threads to avoid blocking the bot's responsiveness during long encoding tasks.

### 7. Global Variables & Configuration (`modules/gvar.py`)
The central configuration hub.
- It loads all environment variables (`os.getenv`).
- It initializes shared singletons like the OpenAI client or the Database instance.
- It defines constants used across the application to avoid magic numbers or hardcoded strings.

## рҹ”§ Deep Dive: Environment Variables

| Variable | Criticality | Technical Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `TOKEN` | **Critical** | The HTTP API Token generated by BotFather. The main `python-telegram-bot` instance uses this for polling and standard API calls. |
| `API_ID` | **Critical** | The Application ID from `my.telegram.org`. Required to initialize the MTProto client (Pyrogram). |
| `API_HASH` | **Critical** | The API Hash paired with the API_ID. Essential for the MTProto handshake. |
| `OPEN_AI` | Optional | API Key for OpenAI. If absent, the `chatgpt.py` module will gracefully fail or disable AI features. |
| `ADMIN` | **Critical** | The numeric Telegram ID of the Superuser. During the boot sequence in `bot.py`, this ID is automatically granted `ADMIN` and `LLM` privileges in the database. |
| `BOT_HANDLER`| Optional | A cleaner prefix for commands (e.g., `.start` vs `/start`). Useful when multiple bots share a group chat to prevent command naming collisions. |
| `HTTP_PROXY` | Optional | Proxy URL. If set, the bot routes traffic through this proxy, useful for hosting in restricted network environments. |

# рҹ‘Ӣ Welcome to VIRUSGAMING64's GitHub Profile ![Snake animation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VIRUSGAMING64/VIRUSGAMING64/output/github-contribution-grid-snake-dark.svg) [![GitHub Metrics](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/VIRUSGAMING64/blob/main/assets/images/github-metrics.svg)](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/VIRUSGAMING64)
--- ## рҹ‘ЁвҖҚрҹ’» About Me I'm a passionate developer and competitive programmer with expertise in multiple programming languages and technologies. I love creating innovative solutions and contributing to the open-source community. ### рҹӣ пёҸ Technical Skills [![skills](https://skillicons.dev/icons?i=cpp,c,cs,py,js,go,java,flask,fastapi,bots,gmail,git,github,vscode,windows,linux,ubuntu&perline=6)](https://skillicons.dev) ### рҹ“Ҡ Language Statistics ![Top Languages](https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api/top-langs/?username=VIRUSGAMING64&langs_count=20&custom_title=VIRUSGAMING64%20LANGS&layout=compact&theme=github_dark) ### рҹҸҶ Codeforces Stats ![Codeforces Stats](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VIRUSGAMING64/cf-stats/main/output/light_card.svg#gh-dark-mode-only) --- ## рҹҡҖ My Projects ### Featured Repositories #### рҹ”§ [Jamazon](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/Jamazon) вӯҗ 9 **Python** | first programing project UH #### рҹҗҡ [vshell](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/vshell) вӯҗ 8 **Python** | No description available #### рҹ”§ [Interpreter](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/Interpreter) вӯҗ 6 **Python** | A simple interpreter #### рҹҗҡ [MPTB_vshell](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/MPTB_vshell) вӯҗ 3 **Python** | Is an new modular python-telegram-bot version of vshell #### рҹҢҗ [virusgaming64.github.io](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io) вӯҗ 1 **CSS** | No description available #### рҹ”§ [VIRUSGAMING64](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/VIRUSGAMING64) вӯҗ 1 **Python** | No description available #### рҹ“Ҡ [cf-stats](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/cf-stats) **Python** | No description available #### рҹҺ“ [final2012c-](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/final2012c-) **C#** | No description available --- ## рҹ“« Connect With Me

repositories.name: virusgaming64.github.io
repositories.readme:
# рҹҺ® VIRUSGAMING64 Portfolio [![GitHub Pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub%20Pages-Live-success?style=for-the-badge&logo=github)](https://virusgaming64.github.io) [![Auto Update](https://img.shields.io/badge/Auto%20Update-Daily-blue?style=for-the-badge&logo=github-actions)](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io/actions) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow?style=for-the-badge)](LICENSE) **A dynamic GitHub portfolio showcasing projects, statistics, and coding journey** [рҹҢҗ Live Demo](https://virusgaming64.github.io) | [рҹ“Ҡ View Stats](https://virusgaming64.github.io) | [рҹҗӣ Report Bug](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io/issues)
--- ## рҹ“– About This Project рҹҢҹ Welcome to my interactive GitHub portfolio! рҹ‘Ӣ This website is a living showcase of my coding projects and development journey. рҹҡҖ It automatically updates daily with the latest repository statistics, programming language usage, and project information directly from my GitHub profile. Built with vanilla JavaScript, HTML, and CSS рҹ’Ә, this portfolio demonstrates clean code practices, API integration, and automation through GitHub Actions. The site features a modern, responsive design with animated backgrounds вңЁ and an intuitive tabbed interface. ### вңЁ Key Features - рҹ”„ **Automated Daily Updates** - Repository statistics refresh automatically every day at 5:40 PM Cuba time (21:40 UTC) - рҹ“Ҡ **Real-Time Repository Stats** - Displays size, stars, forks, and descriptions for all repositories - рҹ’» **Language Analytics** - Visual breakdown of programming languages used across projects - рҹҺЁ **Modern UI/UX** - Clean, responsive interface with animated particle background - рҹ“ұ **Mobile Friendly** - Fully responsive design that works on all devices - вҡЎ **Fast & Lightweight** - No heavy frameworks, just pure vanilla JavaScript - рҹ”Қ **Searchable & Organized** - Easy-to-navigate tabbed layout for repositories and languages --- ## рҹҡҖ Live Demo рҹҢҗ Visit the live website: **[virusgaming64.github.io](https://virusgaming64.github.io)** рҹ‘Ҳ The site features: - **рҹ“Ұ Repos Tab**: Browse all my GitHub repositories with detailed information рҹ“ҡ - **рҹ’» Languages Tab**: See the programming languages I work with and their usage percentages рҹ“Ҳ --- ## рҹӣ пёҸ Technical Architecture вҡҷпёҸ ### How It Works рҹ”§ This portfolio uses a three-component automated system: рҹҺҜ #### 1. рҹӨ– GitHub Actions Workflow Located in `.github/workflows/update-repo-stats.yml`: - Runs automatically on a daily schedule (21:40 UTC) - Can be manually triggered from the Actions tab - Executes the Python data fetcher - Commits updated statistics back to the repository - Triggers automatic deployment to GitHub Pages #### 2. рҹҗҚ Python Data Fetcher Script: `scripts/fetch_repo_stats.py` - Connects to GitHub API to fetch repository data - Retrieves comprehensive information for each repository - Calculates aggregate language statistics - Generates formatted size metrics (KB, MB, GB) - Saves structured data to `data/repo-stats.json` #### 3. рҹҢҗ Interactive Web Interface Files: `index.html`, `src/main.js`, `src/main.css` - Loads and displays repository statistics dynamically - Renders animated particle background for visual appeal - Provides tabbed navigation between different views - Updates in real-time when new data is available - Fully responsive across desktop and mobile devices ### Data Structure рҹ“Ӣ Repository statistics are stored in `data/repo-stats.json`: рҹ’ҫ ```json { "last_updated": "2025-11-08T21:40:00Z", "total_repositories": 15, "overall_languages": { "JavaScript": 45.5, "Python": 30.2, "HTML": 15.3, "CSS": 9.0 }, "repositories": [ { "name": "awesome-project", "description": "An amazing project description", "size_kb": 2048, "size_formatted": "2.0 MB", "languages": { "JavaScript": 70.5, "HTML": 20.0, "CSS": 9.5 }, "stars": 10, "forks": 3, "html_url": "https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/awesome-project", "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z", "updated_at": "2025-11-08T15:20:00Z" } ] } ``` --- ## рҹ’» Local Development рҹ”Ё Want to run this project locally or contribute? Follow these steps: рҹ‘Ү ### Prerequisites вң… - рҹҗҚ Python 3.7 or higher - рҹ”‘ A GitHub Personal Access Token (for fetching repository data) - рҹҢҗ A modern web browser - рҹ“ҡ Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript ### Setup Instructions рҹ“қ 1. **Clone the repository** рҹ“Ҙ ```bash git clone https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io.git cd virusgaming64.github.io ``` 2. **Install Python dependencies** рҹ“Ұ ```bash pip install requests ``` 3. **Set up your GitHub token** рҹ”җ ```bash # Linux/Mac export GITHUB_TOKEN=your_personal_access_token_here # Windows (PowerShell) $env:GITHUB_TOKEN="your_personal_access_token_here" ``` 4. **Fetch repository statistics** рҹ“Ҡ ```bash python scripts/fetch_repo_stats.py ``` 5. **Start a local web server** рҹҡҖ ```bash # Python 3 python -m http.server 8000 # Or use any other local server like Live Server in VS Code ``` 6. **Open your browser** рҹҢҗ Navigate to `http://localhost:8000` рҹ‘Җ ### Manual Statistics Update рҹ”„ To manually update the repository statistics: вҡЎ 1. Navigate to the **[Actions](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io/actions)** tab рҹҺ¬ 2. Select the **"Update Repository Statistics"** workflow рҹ“Ӣ 3. Click **"Run workflow"** button в–¶пёҸ 4. Select the branch (usually `main`) рҹҢҝ 5. Click **"Run workflow"** to confirm вң”пёҸ 6. Wait for the workflow to complete (usually takes 30-60 seconds) вҸұпёҸ 7. Refresh the website to see updated statistics рҹ”ғ --- ## рҹӨқ Contributing рҹ’Ў Contributions, issues, and feature requests are welcome! рҹҺү Feel free to check the [issues page](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io/issues). рҹҗӣ ### How to Contribute рҹҢҹ 1. рҹҚҙ Fork the project 2. рҹҢҝ Create your feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`) 3. рҹ’ҫ Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature'`) 4. рҹҡҖ Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`) 5. рҹҺҒ Open a Pull Request --- ## рҹ“қ License рҹ“ң This project is open source and available under the [MIT License](LICENSE). вҡ–пёҸ --- ## рҹ“¬ Contact рҹ’¬ **VIRUSGAMING64** рҹ‘ЁвҖҚрҹ’» - [@VIRUSGAMING64](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64) Project Link: рҹ”— [https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io](https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/virusgaming64.github.io) ---
**вӯҗ Star this repository if you found it helpful! вӯҗ** Made with вқӨпёҸ and вҳ• by VIRUSGAMING64 рҹҺ® Happy Coding! рҹ’»

repositories.name: vshell
repositories.readme:
# рҹҡҖ vshell

[![License: PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-PolyForm%20Noncommercial%201.0.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.10%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](requirements.txt)

Proyecto modular en Python que combina un nГәcleo de automatizaciГіn (bot) y un componente web, con utilidades para descargas, compresiГіn y gestiГіn de usuarios. Este README documenta la estructura del proyecto, los mГіdulos disponibles y cГіmo ponerlo en marcha.

> в„№пёҸ Nota: Este documento se ha generado a partir de la estructura actual del repositorio. Si tu flujo real de trabajo difiere (por ejemplo, variables de entorno, comandos o servicios especГӯficos), ajГәstalo en la secciГіn ConfiguraciГіn.

---

## рҹ“ҡ Tabla de contenidos
- рҹ“Ұ Requisitos
- вҡҷпёҸ InstalaciГіn
- в–¶пёҸ EjecuciГіn rГЎpida
- рҹ—ӮпёҸ Estructura del proyecto
- рҹ”Ң MГіdulos
- рҹӣ пёҸ Scripts auxiliares
- рҹ§° ConfiguraciГіn
- рҹ‘©вҖҚрҹ’» Desarrollo
- рҹ“қ Problemas conocidos / TODO
- рҹ“„ Licencia

---

## рҹ“Ұ Requisitos

- Python 3.10+ (recomendado)
- pip actualizado
- Entorno virtual (opcional pero recomendado)

Dependencias del proyecto: ver [requirements.txt](requirements.txt).

---

## вҡҷпёҸ InstalaciГіn

1) Clonar el repositorio:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/VIRUSGAMING64/vshell.git
cd vshell
```

2) Crear y activar un entorno virtual (opcional):
- Linux/macOS:
  ```bash
  python -m venv .venv
  source .venv/bin/activate
  ```
- Windows (PowerShell):
  ```powershell
  python -m venv .venv
  .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
  ```

3) Instalar dependencias:
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

---

## в–¶пёҸ EjecuciГіn rГЎpida

Ejecutar el nГәcleo (bot / servicio principal):
```bash
python bot.py
```

> рҹҢҗ Si el proyecto expone un servidor web, revisa el mГіdulo [modules/web.py](modules/web.py) y la carpeta [templates/](templates/) para confirmar el endpoint/puerto y el modo de arranque (puede estar integrado en `bot.py` o levantarse por separado).

---

## рҹ—ӮпёҸ Estructura del proyecto

```text
.
в”ңв”Җ bot.py                  # Punto de entrada del servicio/bot principal
в”ңв”Җ requirements.txt        # Dependencias de Python
в”ңв”Җ TODO                    # Lista de pendientes
в”ңв”Җ modules/                # MГіdulos de negocio y utilidades
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ Gvar.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ IDM.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ Utils.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ VidDown.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ compressor.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ datatypes.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ imports.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ pool.py
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җ users.py
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җ web.py
в”ңв”Җ templates/              # Plantillas utilizadas por el mГіdulo web
в”ңв”Җ web/                    # CГіdigo/recursos del componente web
в”ңв”Җ subc.sh                 # Script auxiliar
в””в”Җ rmbots.sh               # Script auxiliar
```

---

## рҹ”Ң MГіdulos

DescripciГіn del directorio `modules/`:

- рҹ§ӯ Gvar.py
  - GestiГіn de variables globales y configuraciГіn compartida.
  - Centraliza banderas, rutas o parГЎmetros usados por mГәltiples mГіdulos.

- в¬ҮпёҸ IDM.py
  - AbstracciГіn de un gestor de descargas.
  - Maneja colas de descarga, seguimiento de progreso y reintentos.

- рҹ§° Utils.py
  - Utilidades comunes y funciones de apoyo transversales (I/O, validaciones, formateo, logging, etc.).
  - Evita duplicaciГіn de cГіdigo con helpers reutilizables.

- рҹҺҘ VidDown.py
  - LГіgica de descarga de videos desde distintas fuentes/plataformas.
  - Envuelve bibliotecas de descarga y normaliza la salida/formato.

- рҹ—ңпёҸ compressor.py
  - CompresiГіn/descompresiГіn de ficheros y directorios (p. ej., ZIP/TAR).
  - Manejo de tamaГұos, divisiГіn de archivos y rutas temporales.

- рҹ§© datatypes.py
  - Tipos, constantes y estructuras del dominio.
  - Ayuda al tipado y contratos internos entre mГіdulos.

- рҹ’Ө imports.py
  - GestiГіn de imports opcionales y carga perezosa (lazy loading).
  - Mejora compatibilidad y tiempos de arranque.

- вҡЎ pool.py
  - Pool de ejecuciГіn (hilos/procesos/tareas asГӯncronas) para trabajos en paralelo.
  - Ideal para operaciones intensivas de red/IO, descargas y tareas batch.

- рҹ‘Ө users.py
  - GestiГіn de usuarios: autorizaciГіn, perfiles/estados y persistencia asociada.
  - Puede incluir listas blanca/negra, roles o lГӯmites por usuario.

- рҹҢҗ web.py
  - Servidor web/API y rutas.
  - Integra recursos de [templates/](templates/) y posiblemente [web/](web/).

---

## рҹӣ пёҸ Scripts auxiliares

- рҹ§Ә subc.sh
  - Script de apoyo para tareas de desarrollo/operaciГіn. Revisa su contenido para el uso exacto.

- рҹ§№ rmbots.sh
  - Script de apoyo minimalista. Revisa su contenido para el uso exacto.

> Asegura permisos de ejecuciГіn:
```bash
chmod +x subc.sh rmbots.sh
```

---

## рҹ§° ConfiguraciГіn

La configuraciГіn depende de cГіmo `bot.py` y `modules/web.py` consuman parГЎmetros. Patrones comunes:

- Variables de entorno (ejemplos):
  - `BOT_TOKEN`, `API_ID`, `API_HASH`, `ADMINS`
  - Rutas de salida para descargas/compresiГіn
  - ParГЎmetros del servidor web (`HOST`, `PORT`)

- Archivos de configuraciГіn:
  - Si se usa, puede ser `.env`, YAML o JSON.

Ejemplos:

Linux/macOS:
```bash
export BOT_TOKEN="TU_TOKEN"
export PORT=8080
```

Windows (PowerShell):
```powershell
$env:BOT_TOKEN="TU_TOKEN"
$env:PORT=8080
```

---

## рҹ‘©вҖҚрҹ’» Desarrollo

- Estilo de cГіdigo:
  - MantГ©n funciones utilitarias en `Utils.py`.
  - Define tipos/constantes en `datatypes.py`.
  - Usa `pool.py` para tareas concurrentes.
  - Centraliza configuraciГіn compartida en `Gvar.py`.

- AГұadir un nuevo mГіdulo:
  1) Crea un archivo en `modules/` con una responsabilidad clara.
  2) Exporta funciones/clases pГәblicas bien definidas.
  3) Integra el mГіdulo en `bot.py` o donde corresponda.
  4) Documenta brevemente el mГіdulo en este README.

- Plantillas/UI:
  - `web.py` utiliza `templates/`. MantГ©n lГіgica y presentaciГіn separadas.

---

## рҹ“қ Problemas conocidos / TODO

Consulta [TODO](TODO) para la lista actual de pendientes. Si aГұades tareas nuevas, actualiza ese archivo y/o crea issues.

---

## рҹ“„ Licencia

Este proyecto estГЎ licenciado bajo PolyForm Noncommercial License 1.0.0. Puedes usar, modificar y redistribuir el cГіdigo para fines no comerciales. No se permite vender este software ni usarlo con fines comerciales.

- SPDX: `PolyForm-Noncommercial-1.0.0`
- Texto completo: https://polyformproject.org/licenses/noncommercial/1.0.0/
